# Cache — Catalog

## Overview

Redis client management. Owns the singleton `ioredis` client used by domain-level caches (permission cache, idempotency, rate-limiter), the BullMQ queue connection (re-exported by [src/infrastructure/queue/connection.ts](src/infrastructure/queue/connection.ts)), and the URL parser that normalises managed-Redis URLs (Railway, Upstash) into the connection options BullMQ expects.

→ Read the full overview: [OVERVIEW.md](./OVERVIEW.md)

## Clients

### `bullmqRedisConnection`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/bullmq-redis.client.ts`

Dedicated Redis connection for BullMQ health probes when an override endpoint is configured.
BullMQ queues use {@link getBullMQConnectionOptions} — not this client's keyPrefix.

### `closeBullMqRedis`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/bullmq-redis.client.ts`

<!-- MISSING_DESCRIPTION -->

### `connectBullMqRedis`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/bullmq-redis.client.ts`

Eagerly connects the BullMQ Redis client when it uses a separate endpoint from cache Redis.

### `closeRedis`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis.client.ts`

<!-- MISSING_DESCRIPTION -->

### `connectRedis`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis.client.ts`

Eagerly establishes the shared Redis connection and waits until it is ready.

Combined with `lazyConnect: true` and `enableOfflineQueue: false`, the very
first command in a process would otherwise race the initial connect and
fail with "Stream isn't writeable and enableOfflineQueue options is false".

Call once at process start (HTTP server, worker) before serving traffic or
registering schedulers. Idempotent and safe to call when already connected.

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis.client.ts`

## Utilities

### `resolveRedisKeyPrefix`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-prefix.util.ts`

Resolves the Redis key prefix for cache, idempotency, rate limits, and BullMQ.
Defaults to `core:<NODE_ENV>:` when REDIS_KEY_PREFIX is unset.

### `warnWhenBullMqSharesCacheRedisHost`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-topology-warn.util.ts`

Logs a warning when BullMQ uses a separate Redis endpoint while single-instance
Redis is the expected topology.

### `deriveBullMqRedisUrlFromCacheUrl`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-url.parse.util.ts`

When REDIS_BULLMQ_URL is unset, BullMQ shares REDIS_URL.

### `isRedisTlsUrl`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-url.parse.util.ts`

True when the Redis URL explicitly uses TLS (`rediss://`).

### `resolveRedisHostFromUrl`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-url.parse.util.ts`

Hostname from a Redis URL (no env dependency).

### `usesSeparateBullMqRedisEndpoint`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-url.parse.util.ts`

True when cache and BullMQ use different logical databases or hosts.

### `usesSeparateBullMqRedisHost`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-url.parse.util.ts`

True when cache and BullMQ URLs resolve to different Redis hosts.

### `validateProductionRedisTopology`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-url.parse.util.ts`

Production topology currently allows a single shared Redis instance.

### `resolveBullMqRedisHost`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-url.util.ts`

BullMQ Redis hostname from resolved BullMQ URL.

### `resolveBullMqRedisUrl`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-url.util.ts`

BullMQ Redis URL — explicit REDIS_BULLMQ_URL or the shared REDIS_URL.

### `resolveCacheRedisHost`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-url.util.ts`

Cache Redis hostname from {@link env.REDIS_URL}.

### `usesSeparateBullMqRedisDatabase`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-url.util.ts`

True when cache and BullMQ use different logical databases or hosts (env-bound).

### `usesSeparateBullMqRedisHostFromEnv`

Source: `src/infrastructure/cache/redis-url.util.ts`

True when cache and BullMQ use different Redis hosts (env-bound).
